chronos 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    Bibliography

14C Calibration

Source: R/AllGenerics.R, R/radiocarbon.R
c14_calibrate.Rd

Calibrates radiocarbon dates.

Usage

c14_calibrate(ages, errors, ...)

# S4 method for numeric,numeric
c14_calibrate(
  ages,
  errors,
  names = NULL,
  curves = "intcal20",
  reservoir_offsets = 0,
  reservoir_errors = 0,
  from = 55000,
  to = 0,
  resolution = 1,
  normalize = TRUE,
  F14C = FALSE,
  drop = TRUE,
  eps = 1e-06
)

Arguments

ages

A numeric vector giving the BP ages to be calibrated.

errors

A numeric vector giving the standard deviation of the ages to be calibrated.

...

Currently not used.

names

A character vector specifying the names of the ages (e.g. laboratory codes).

curves

A character vector specifying the calibration curve to be used. Different curves can be specified per dated sample.

reservoir_offsets

A numeric vector giving the offset values for any marine reservoir effect (defaults to 0; i.e. no offset).

reservoir_errors

A numeric vector giving the offset value errors for any marine reservoir effect (defaults to 0; i.e. no offset).

from

length-one numeric vector specifying the earliest data to calibrate for, in cal. BP years.

to

A length-one numeric vector specifying the latest data to calibrate for, in cal. BP years.

resolution

A length-one numeric vector specifying the temporal resolution (in years) of the calibration.

normalize

A logical scalar: should the calibration be normalized?

F14C

A logical scalar: should the calibration be carried out in F14C space?

drop

A logical scalar: should years with zero probability be discarded? If TRUE (the default), results in a narrower time range.

eps

A length-one numeric value giving the cutoff below which calibration values will be removed.

Value

A CalibratedAges object.

Note

Adapted from rcarbon::calibrate() by Andrew Bevan and Enrico Crema.

References

Bronk Ramsey, C. (2008). RADIOCARBON DATING: REVOLUTIONS IN UNDERSTANDING. Archaeometry, 50:249-275. doi:10.1111/j.1475-4754.2008.00394.x .

See also

Other radiocarbon tools: F14C, c14_combine(), c14_curve(), c14_plot, spd()

Author

N. Frerebeau

Examples

## Calibrate a single date
cal <- c14_calibrate(300, 20)
plot(cal)


## Calibrate multiple dates
cal <- c14_calibrate(
  ages = c(5000, 4500),
  errors = c(45, 35),
  names = c("X", "Y")
)
plot(cal, panel.first = graphics::grid())


plot(cal, density = TRUE, interval = FALSE)

plot(cal, density = FALSE, interval = TRUE)


## Convert BP scale to CE
CE <- project(cal, "CE")
plot(CE)


## Convert BP scale to b2k
b2k <- project(cal, "b2k")
plot(b2k)

On this page

chronos is a part of the tesselle project, a collection of packages for research and teaching in archaeology. Learn more at tesselle.org.

Developed by Nicolas Frerebeau.
